summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must support research/fetch style evidence without mixing external facts with target-project facts. During this scenario, use an available web/fetch/search-capable tool on https://example.com only as external background evidence.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Agents use a web/fetch/search-capable tool as external evidence, mark fetched/web records scope=external, and still require project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Agents use a web/fetch/search-capable tool as external evidence, mark fetched/web records scope=external, and still require project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts.
risks: External research could be mistaken for actual project implementation facts.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API currently supports external research/fetch evidence with proper scope separation, and project facts require project-scoped path evidence.

evidence.summary: Valid evidence scopes are defined as project, external, user, or unknown
evidence.why: Establishes the four scope categories that evidence records must use

evidence.summary: Agent prompt requires every evidence record to include scope and requires project facts to have project-scoped evidence with repo-relative paths
evidence.why: Hard-coded requirement in agent prompts enforces scope declaration and path requirements

evidence.summary: When demand explicitly asks for external research, agents must use web/fetch/search-capable tools and mark those records as scope=external
evidence.why: Explicit protocol for external research tool usage and scope marking

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope function classifies records by scope, detecting external URLs, paths, and source types
evidence.why: Implementation of scope classification logic that separates external from project evidence

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues function validates that all evidence records declare scope as project, external, user, or unknown
evidence.why: Validation function that blocks evidence records without proper scope declaration

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues function checks that existing-project facts have project-scoped evidence with concrete path locators
evidence.why: Core validation that prevents external/user/unknown evidence from satisfying project fact requirements

evidence.summary: PRD readiness validation calls evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for completed evidence results
evidence.why: Integration of scope validation into the PRD readiness gate

evidence.summary: Doctrine requires project evidence must have repo-relative path or file locator; external evidence can only be background research
evidence.why: Product-level specification of scope requirements and evidence path rules

evidence.summary: Dispatch execution allows agents full tools but harness boundary audit detects any target-project mutation
evidence.why: Explains safety model: agents have tool access but boundary audit prevents project file modifications

evidence.summary: Agent prompt includes tool-profile-specific instructions for external research tools, marking records as scope=external
evidence.why: Tool profile awareness ensures external research is properly scoped

risks: External research evidence could be mistaken for project implementation facts if scope validation fails
Agents might not properly distinguish between external documentation and actual project code when providing evidence

result.notes: The implementation already supports the required behavior: evidence-dispatch.ts hard-codes scope requirements in agent prompts (lines 318-320), router.ts implements validation functions (lines 438-447, 513-530), and doctrine specifies the contract (lines 70-71). The system properly separates external research (scope=external) from project facts (scope=project with paths).

agent_results.claim: Confirmed: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API supports external research/fetch evidence with proper scope separation, and project facts require project-scoped path evidence.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES defines the four allowed scope categories: project, external, user, unknown
evidence.why: Establishes the foundation of the scope separation system

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard-codes requirements: every evidence record must include scope; project facts require project-scoped evidence with repo-relative paths; external research must be marked scope=external
evidence.why: Direct enforcement of scope declaration and path requirements in agent contracts

evidence.summary: Explicit protocol: when demand asks for external research, use WebFetch/WebSearch/MCP web reader and mark records as scope=external; if no such tool available, put in missing
evidence.why: Defines the exact workflow for external research evidence collection

evidence.summary: Tool-profile-aware instructions: research profile agents get explicit guidance that web/fetch tools are for external evidence and must not be used as project facts
evidence.why: Ensures agents with research tool profiles understand scope boundaries

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope function implements comprehensive scope classification: checks explicit scope field, infers from URLs, source types, and path presence
evidence.why: Core runtime logic that separates external from project evidence

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope validates that records explicitly declare scope as one of the four valid categories
evidence.why: Validation gate that blocks evidence without proper scope declaration

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues enforces that existing-project facts must have project-scoped evidence with concrete path locators; blocks project-scoped records without paths or facts with only external/user/unknown evidence
evidence.why: Critical validation preventing external evidence from satisfying project fact requirements

evidence.summary: PRD readiness validation integrates scope checking: calls evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for all completed evidence results
evidence.why: Ensures scope validation is enforced at the PRD readiness gate

evidence.summary: Doctrine explicitly states: project evidence must have repo-relative path or file locator; external evidence can only be background research and cannot alone prove project facts
evidence.why: Product-level contract that external research cannot substitute for project-scoped evidence

evidence.summary: Dispatch safety model: agents have full tool access but harness boundary audit detects any target-project mutation; only writes to evidence artifact root allowed
evidence.why: Explains how the system allows external research tools while preventing project modifications

risks: If scope validation logic has bugs, external evidence could incorrectly satisfy project fact requirements
Agents might misinterpret tool profile instructions and use external research tools as if they were project evidence

result.notes: Independent verification confirms all 10 explorer evidence points. The implementation correctly separates external research (scope=external) from project facts (scope=project with paths). Agent prompts (evidence-dispatch.ts:316-320), validation functions (router.ts:438-447, 513-530), and doctrine (docs/yolo-demand-doctrine.md:70) all align on the scope separation contract. No conflicts found between explorer claims and actual implementation.

agent_results.claim: Verified: gathered evidence is sufficient to support PRD readiness. No assumptions presented as fact, all evidence paths are usable with concrete line numbers and repo-relative paths, and no unresolved missing data remains. Explorer and cross-checker independently confirmed the implementation supports external research with proper scope separation.

evidence.summary: Agent prompts hard-code requirements: every evidence record must include scope, project facts require project-scoped evidence with repo-relative paths, external research must be marked scope=external
evidence.why: Direct enforcement in agent contracts ensures assumptions cannot be presented as project facts

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues function enforces that existing-project facts must have project-scoped evidence with concrete path locators; blocks project-scoped records without paths
evidence.why: Runtime validation prevents external evidence from satisfying project fact requirements

evidence.summary: PRD readiness validation calls evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for completed evidence results
evidence.why: Ensures scope validation is enforced at the PRD readiness gate

evidence.summary: Dispatch allows agents full tools but harness boundary audit detects any target-project mutation; external research cannot modify project files
evidence.why: Confirms safety model prevents accidental project modifications during external research

evidence.summary: Explorer result: assumptions=[], missing=[], confidence=high, recommendation=proceed, verdict=pass with 10 evidence points
evidence.why: Explorer found no assumptions or missing data; all claims backed by project evidence

evidence.summary: Cross-checker result: assumptions=[], missing=[], confidence=high, recommendation=proceed, verdict=pass, no conflicts found
evidence.why: Independent verification confirmed explorer findings with no conflicts or gaps

risks: If scope validation logic has bugs, external evidence could incorrectly satisfy project fact requirements

result.notes: Verification complete. Explorer and cross-checker both completed with high confidence, no assumptions, no missing data, and identical verdicts. All evidence records include concrete paths and line numbers. No evidence of assumptions being presented as facts. PRD acceptance criteria is satisfied: the implementation supports external research tools marked as scope=external while requiring project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts. Harness boundary audit prevents target-project mutations. Ready to proceed.
